quadrant.title: Intelligence per dollar: all major models at a glance
quadrant.intro: Each dot is a model. Further right means more expensive (price per 1M tokens, blended 3:1 input:output, log scale), further up means smarter (Intelligence Index from Artificial Analysis, v4.1). Top left is the sweet spot.
quadrant.hinweis: Prices = official API list prices (excluding batch/cache), Index = Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 in the configuration tested there (usually highest reasoning). As of: 07/17/2026 — numbers change fast.

zones.label: Frontier
zones.desc: Maximum capability — when the outcome matters more than the price.

zones.label: Price-performance
zones.desc: Lots of intelligence for the money — the everyday zone for most tasks.

zones.label: Budget
zones.desc: Cheap for scale, simple tasks, and experiments — often open weights.

ratio.title: The ranking: intelligence per dollar
ratio.intro: Intelligence Index divided by the blended price (3 parts input + 1 part output, per 1M tokens). Higher = more thinking power per dollar. This says nothing about the absolute top spot — that's what the index itself is for.
fussnoten: Grok 4.5: price applies up to 200K context — above that it doubles (ratio then ~9.0).
Gemini 3.1 Pro: price applies up to a 200K prompt — above that $4/$18 (ratio then ~6.2).
Qwen3.7-Max: currently a time-limited 50% discount (ratio then ~24.5) — the list price counts here.
Mistral Large 3 ($0.50/$1.50) and Llama 4 Maverick (from ~$0.27/$0.85 at hosters) are missing from the ranking because Artificial Analysis doesn't list an index for them.
Sources: official provider pricing pages + artificialanalysis.ai, all accessed on 07/17/2026.

modelleIntro: The current models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI — and now also Moonshot (Kimi), DeepSeek, Zhipu (GLM), Meta, Alibaba (Qwen), and Mistral. With price, context, strengths, and what they're meant for.

modelle.name: Kimi K3
modelle.positionierung: Brand new (07/16/2026): according to Moonshot, the largest open-weight model in the world (2.8 trillion parameters, MoE) — rank 3 in the Intelligence Index, right behind Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol.
modelle.preis: $3 / $15 per MTok, cache-hit input $0.30 (as of: July 2026)
stark: Intelligence Index 57.11 — rank 3 of all models (Artificial Analysis, 07/17/2026)
Agentic web research: BrowseComp 91.2 (Moonshot's own figure)
Flat price across the entire 1M context, automatic caching
schwach: As of 07/17/2026, weights are NOT yet downloadable — open release announced only for 07/27/2026
Thinking is permanently on — fast, cheap answers aren't its thing
modelle.wofuer: Use it for demanding agent and research tasks when you want frontier performance cheaper than Fable/Sol.

modelle.name: Kimi K2.5
modelle.positionierung: The predecessor: 1-trillion-parameter MoE, open weights (Modified MIT), still available and significantly cheaper.
modelle.preis: $0.60 / $3 per MTok, cache hit $0.10 (as of: July 2026)
stark: SWE-bench Verified 76.8% (Moonshot's own figure) at a budget price
Open weights — self-hostable
Multimodal + agentic search
schwach: Index 35.37 — noticeably below K3 and the frontier models
modelle.wofuer: Use it for cheap agentic coding or self-hosting with decent performance.

modelle.name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
modelle.positionierung: The price-performance king of this overview: intelligence per dollar unmatched (ratio 81) — open weights under MIT license.
modelle.preis: $0.435 / $0.87 per MTok, cache-hit input ~99% cheaper (as of: July 2026)
stark: By far the best intelligence-per-dollar ratio (index 44.27 at $0.54 blended)
SWE-bench Verified 80.6% (DeepSeek's own figure, Pro-Max mode)
OpenAI- AND Anthropic-compatible API, open weights (MIT)
schwach: Absolute top-end performance is below the frontier models
Benchmark numbers are vendor-reported, not independently validated
modelle.wofuer: Use it if you want to process a lot of volume cheaply — or want to self-host a strong model.

modelle.name: GLM-4.7
modelle.positionierung: China's open-source workhorse for coding: 358B MoE under MIT license, strong at agentic coding and frontend generation.
modelle.preis: $0.60 / $2.20 per MTok; Flash variant free (as of: July 2026)
stark: SWE-bench Verified 73.8% (Zhipu's own figure) at a budget price
Open weights (MIT) — self-hosting via vLLM/SGLang, 44 quantized variants
Free Flash variant to try out
schwach: Index 33.70 — others take the crown for complex reasoning
Zhipu already has a newer GLM-5 series — 4.7 is no longer the house's flagship model
modelle.wofuer: Use it for cheap agentic coding, web/frontend generation, or as a self-hosting base.

modelle.name: Muse Spark 1.1
modelle.positionierung: Meta's first closed-weight frontier model (07/09/2026) — via the new Meta Model API, built for agentic orchestration across apps, MCP servers, and skills.
modelle.preis: $1.25 / $4.25 per MTok, cached input $0.15 (as of: July 2026)
stark: Index 50.62 at just $2 blended — fourth-best intelligence-per-dollar ratio
Agentic orchestration (apps/MCP/skills, zero-shot) as core positioning
OpenAI-SDK-compatible API, $20 starting credit
schwach: NOT open-weights — a break with Meta's Llama tradition
Public benchmarks partly published only as chart images
modelle.wofuer: Use it for multi-app agents and tool orchestration at a good price.

modelle.name: Llama 4 Maverick
modelle.positionierung: Meta's current open-weights flagship (400B MoE, natively multimodal) — runs extremely cheap at hosters like Together, Groq, or Fireworks.
modelle.preis: from ~$0.27 / $0.85 per MTok depending on hoster (Together AI, as of: July 2026)
stark: Cheapest entry in this overview
Open weights + self-hosting
Multimodal, 12 languages
schwach: No Artificial Analysis index listed — limited comparability
The Llama 4 Community License is not OSI-compliant (restrictions apply above 700M users)
modelle.wofuer: Use it for mass processing at minimal cost or your own hosting.

modelle.name: Qwen3.7-Max
modelle.positionierung: Alibaba's agent flagship ("designed for the agent era") — proprietary for the first time instead of open-weight, with hybrid reasoning at the same price.
modelle.preis: $2.50 / $7.50 per MTok list price; currently temporarily −50% (as of: July 2026)
stark: SWE-bench Verified 80.4% and GPQA Diamond 92.4 (Alibaba's own figures)
Long-horizon agentic work across hundreds of steps as core focus
Very strong multilingual performance (WMT24++ 85.8)
schwach: No self-hosting — a break with the Qwen open-weight tradition
Index 45.99 is below the Western frontier models
modelle.wofuer: Use it for long agent chains and multilingual tasks — especially during the discount promotion.

modelle.name: Mistral Large 3
modelle.positionierung: Europe's open-weights flagship (675B MoE, Apache 2.0) — vision, function calling, and an EU hosting option.
modelle.preis: $0.50 / $1.50 per MTok (model page; an older FAQ still lists $2/$6) (as of: July 2026)
stark: Apache 2.0 open weights — the freest license among the big players
Very cheap for its size
EU hosting/data sovereignty
schwach: Benchmark numbers mostly published only as chart images
modelle.wofuer: Use it if open source, EU hosting, or the Apache license are decisive for you.

modelle.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
modelle.positionierung: Mistral's coding specialist: 128B dense, Modified MIT, with a strong SWE-bench score for its size.
stark: SWE-bench Verified 77.6% (Mistral's own figure)
Open weights (Modified MIT)
Compact enough for your own hosting
schwach: Index 29.95 — weaker than the MoE giants for broad reasoning
modelle.wofuer: Use it for coding agents on your own infrastructure.
